events.title: Classical Music on the Green
events.description: An intimate classical guitar concert on the green, positioned as the neighborhood's first music event following Temperance Alley's renovation. Attendees invited to bring companions, blankets or chairs, and suggestions for future alley programming.

events.title: Sidewalk Palooza
events.description: An event addressing pedestrian infrastructure issues in the neighborhood. Participants gather at Temperance Alley to document infrastructure needs, submit 311 requests for sidewalk repairs, and participate in community chalk art activities. Organizers note that while potholes receive rapid city attention (3 business days average), sidewalk requests take approximately 270 days to fulfill, disproportionately affecting people with disabilities, parents with strollers, and seniors. Additional information at sidewalkpalooza.org.

events.title: Alley Garden Salon: Musings on Love and Kindness
events.description: Participants share poetry, texts, or personal reflections on love and kindness through reading, discussion, writing, and creative exchange, weaving a "collective mind garden." Flexible attendance welcomed; bring pen, paper, and optional poems or texts.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden
events.description: A complimentary yoga session led by local teacher Aabi in the garden. Extra mats will be on hand for those who don't have their own.

events.title: Silent Disco / The Morning Musical Multiverse: An Immersive Audio Experience in Temperance Alley Garden
events.description: Attendees choose between mindful movement set to ambient soundscapes or an energetic dance experience, either lying back to gaze at the clouds or dancing with fellow early risers. Complimentary wireless headphones are supplied. The event includes coffee and tea beforehand, plus bagels, watermelon, and sparkling water afterward. Casual or creative attire is encouraged (pajamas, neon/tie-dye, disco costumes, or formal wear all welcome). This was a rescheduled event, replacing a previous Friday-night Silent Disco that had sold out but was rained out; forecast was sunny in the low 70s.
events.possibleExceptionDescription: Rescheduled after rain-out, prior date sold out

events.title: USNA New Member Mingle
events.description: USNA's inaugural New Member Mingle in the Temperance Alley Garden, featuring food and drink, an opportunity to get to know each other, fun artifacts from the USNA archives, and brainstorming ideas for improving the neighborhood. New members receive a welcome packet with association history and garden access information, and can harvest herbs (mint, tarragon, sage, basil, thyme, oregano, and more) to take home.

events.title: How to See a Flower
events.description: Guided spiritual ecology practices in the shared garden, teaching the art of close observation and how to be in relationship with garden plants. All ages welcome.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden

events.title: How to See a Plant: Flowers
events.description: Guided ecology practices in the shared garden designed to help participants overcome plant blindness, learning observation techniques and developing a deeper connection with garden plants.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden
events.description: Complimentary yoga session in the garden with instructor Xandra Scott. Extra mats on hand for those who don't have their own.

events.title: Native Land Acknowledgment Ceremony for Temperance Alley
events.description: Formal ceremony recognizing Native Anacostan peoples and African-American residents as traditional stewards and former inhabitants of the area. Includes drumming from the African-American community and a presentation by Armand Lione, director of the DC Native History Project, on the land's significance to the Anacostan Indians. A permanent sign acknowledging the Anacostan people, the Piscataway Indian Nation, and residents from the 1800s-1900s will be installed.

events.title: Harvest Moon Celebration
events.description: Celebration of the Harvest Moon, the full moon falling closest to the Autumnal Equinox, whose moonlight aids harvest of summer crops in the northern hemisphere. Activities include watching the moon rise while enjoying cider and moon cakes.

events.title: and not by what we call ourselves: Creative Nonfiction Writing Workshop
events.description: Writing workshop inviting participants to explore and introduce themselves through their writing, coming with a version of self they want to ask questions about.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden
events.description: Free yoga in the garden with local teacher Aabi. A few extra mats will be on hand for those without their own.

events.title: Autumnal Equinox Celebration
events.description: Celebration of the autumn equinox, when "the sun passes most directly over the equator and the day and night are of equal length." Neighbors are invited to join in the garden at dusk to share in harvest snacks.

events.title: How to See a Plant: Vegetables
events.description: Guided ecology practice in the shared backyard garden, designed to help participants overcome plant blindness by learning close observation and how to be in relationship with the garden's vegetable plants.

events.title: Art of Memory Workshop
events.description: A session exploring memorization as an artistic practice, beginning with an overview of memorization techniques—how they were invented and how they developed historically—then examining the connection between architecture and shared memory while practicing use of the garden as a memory palace.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden
events.description: Free yoga class starting with pranayama, building into a dynamic flow class incorporating free movement, and ending with deep relaxation; variations given to support all levels. Extra mats provided for those without their own.

events.title: Framing Flora: Garden Photography at Temperance Alley
events.description: Photography workshop where participants learn about the garden's history and garden photography techniques; a special guest horticulturist shares insights and tips. Open to photographers of all levels using any device (phone camera, DSLR, film). Attendees may share photos, with selected images featured in a later outdoor installation.

events.title: Alley Garden Salon: On Forgiveness & Grace
events.description: Evening gathering exploring the power of forgiveness and grace, including reading passages from religious and philosophical sources, individual journaling, and group dialogue. Attendees invited to bring meaningful objects, poetry, or personal stories related to the theme; garden lit with candles. Agenda: opening grounding exercise, readings, individual reflection, and group discussion.

events.title: How to See a Plant: Trees
events.description: Guided exercises in the shared backyard designed to help participants overcome plant blindness, engaging in observational practices and relationship-building with garden plants in a casual atmosphere. Part of an ongoing workshop series.

events.title: Pumpkin Carving
events.description: An autumn gathering inviting community members to carve pumpkins together. Organizers provide fall snacks, candles, and limited extra supplies, though attendees are encouraged to bring their own pumpkins and carving tools or stencils.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden
events.description: Free all-levels yoga flow class in the garden with local instructors, open to any experience level. A few extra mats are available for those who don't have their own.

events.title: Mystical Tales: A Writing Workshop
events.description: Writing workshop focused on fantasy, horror, magical realism, folklore, and mythology, using writing to 'cast spells with our words to change what we fear, resent, and ignore in our lives and in our worlds.' Writing materials provided; participants may bring their own. Includes optional sharing time at the end. Outdoor garden event lit by blankets, candles, and streetlights; attendees should dress warmly.
events.possibleExceptionDescription: One-time workshop, not recurring series

events.title: Ecstatic Dance in the Garden
events.description: A community dance gathering in silent disco format (headphones provided) emphasizing conscious connection, movement, and music in a non-verbal, alcohol-free, barefoot space. events.title: "When the Earth Started to Sing" a sonic journey
events.description: A communal listening gathering in the garden at sunset to experience "This sonic journey written and narrated by David G. Haskell brings us to the beginning of sound and song on planet Earth." The piece, made entirely of tiny trembling waves in air, combines spoken words with terrestrial sounds, inviting listeners into an experience of the living Earth and its history, and exploring how the chorus of modern sounds—from forest to oceans to human music—emerged from life's community. The event invites attendees on "a journey into deep time and deep sound that will open your ears and your imagination."

events.title: Preserving Abundance: A Writing Workshop for the Autumnal Equinox

The event emphasizes 'question driven learning' to make mathematics more accessible and less intimidating for participants interested in gardening and math.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden
events.description: An inclusive yoga session in a garden setting welcoming participants of all experience levels for an hour of yoga under the garden sky. Bring a mat and water.

events.title: Breathe Easy: Group Meditation
events.description: A recurring weekly meditation gathering, "Wednesdays at dusk," featuring inner spaciousness practices, breath work, and guided loving kindness meditations.

events.title: Donation Harvest for Martha's Table
events.description: Our midweek harvest is donated to Martha's Table through a long-running partnership with Farm the District. Join us every hump day at 7 to beat the heat and give back to the community. No experience necessary, we'll teach you how to harvest.

events.title: Creatives Club & Community Jam
events.description: Combines TAG zine distribution, open songwriting hours, and Creatives Club DC activities. Attendees bring works in progress or start something new; the songwriting session invites help creating original music for a community mixtape to be recorded and produced in fall, using their own instruments or garden items. Creatives Club DC meets Thursdays 6-9pm at various city locations as a space for DMV creatives to socialize over low-key art-making. Mixtape participation requires inquiring via a linked form.
events.possibleExceptionDescription: Special zine release combined with songwriting hours

events.title: Super Harvest Moon Garden Celebration
events.description: Fall Equinox gathering with a veggie-friendly cookout, garden tarot, reflective poems and prompts, letters to future selves, and interactive celestial demonstrations. Attendees invited to bring items for grilling. Transitions into the separate Music in the Garden event at 7:30 PM, featuring the Abe Mamet duo.
events.possibleExceptionDescription: One-time equinox event transitioning to separate concert

events.title: Music in the Garden! ft the Abe Mamet duo
events.description: An acoustic evening featuring a french horn and bass jazz duo, joining the garden plants and critters for an evening of home-grown music, held every Final Friday of the Month, described as somewhere between a house show and barnyard jam.

events.title: Garden Volunteer Hours
events.description: Community volunteer session tending shared crops, filling water barrels, and working on side projects together. Attendees take home fresh produce; newcomers and first-time visitors welcome.

events.title: Art All Night!
events.description: Evening celebration at Temperance Alley Garden with live music starting at 5 PM, a gallery exhibition at 6 PM showcasing local artists Kia Green and Warren Henson, and a fashion show at 8 PM. Visitors invited to meet the Garden Stewards and learn about creative projects grown over the past 3 years.
events.possibleExceptionDescription: Annual one-night arts event, not weekly series

events.title: 2 QuTe 4 COViD Safety: an outdoor Masked Mixer
events.description: Alcohol-free outdoor mixer centering Two Spirit, queer, trans, and intersex folks focused on COVID awareness and mutual care. Honor-system attendance requires 10-14 days of precautions (masking on transit, pre-arrival testing); potluck-style food in disposable containers; N95/KN95 masks required. Open to all, with organizers welcoming accessibility questions.

events.description: A potluck gathering in the Garden's Sukkah to celebrate Sukkot, the Jewish harvest festival. Attendees share a meal, reflect together, and connect with community members; the event welcomes everyone regardless of background or prior knowledge of the holiday. Guests are asked to bring something vegetarian to share and label dishes with allergens.

events.title: DC Sing-Along Night
events.description: A contemporary take on traditional sing-alongs blending the communal spirit of '60s sing-alongs with a modern songbook and "rawk 'tude." Participants can bring acoustic instruments to join the band (all skill levels welcome) or simply sing along, calling out favorite songs from the provided songbook spanning classic and contemporary artists. Children are welcome, though the event contains mild profanity.

events.title: BUGS under BRICKS: A Sunday Playtest
events.description: A return of the garden's original "How2See" ecology curriculum. Participants pick up bricks to observe the life underneath, then share observations with the group. Part of an ongoing October Sunday series; listed as playtesting in progress.

events.title: Garden Futures Forum
events.description: A forum convening organizational partners, event hosts, and association members to discuss the possibility of a lease extension on the Garden for another year, and begin a month-long exploration into the potential for extended programming and operations. All are welcome to weigh in, regardless of past involvement; participants are encouraged to bring questions, ideas, and commitments.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden
events.description: An all-levels yoga session in a garden environment, emphasizing grounding and centering. Bring a mat and water and join for an hour of yoga under the garden sky.

events.title: Gong Bath for Seasons Change
events.description: An immersive sound healing experience featuring gong vibrations designed to induce meditative states, marking the change of seasons. Participants should bring a mat and water bottle. Wowsugi's practice encompasses photography, performance, sound healing, and horticulture, focusing on belonging and community building.

events.title: Breathe Easy: Group Meditation
events.description: A recurring Wednesday evening gathering, "the regular hang, Wednesdays at dusk," featuring inner spaciousness practices, breath work, and guided loving kindness meditations.

events.description: Informational gathering inviting families to learn about the Parent and Alumni Leadership Council (PALC) of the DC Public Charter School Board, which delivers community advice on education policy affecting DC public charter schools. Current members and staff discuss their involvement; pizza and non-alcoholic beverages provided.

Part of Creatives Club's recurring Thursday evening program.

events.title: Midday Vibing
events.description: An informal gathering offering attendees a chance to chill out in the garden and reconnect with nature and community during the work day. events.title: Garden Volunteer Hours

This is diy/experimental mending rather than professional tailoring, embracing upcycling and mending old ripped jeans with love and care in the Garden.

events.title: Cloud Watching Club
events.description: An evening gathering focused on "appreciating the sky while letting the clouds roll by." Attendees view the sunset in the garden's "big sky preserve" and may bring cloud-themed poetry or artwork to share. Paper towel roll monoscopes will be available for participants.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden

events.title: Snailmail Station x Offline Night: a Sunday Playtest
events.location: 1909 13th Street Northwest, Washington, DC 20009
events.description: A communal gathering to practice the art of letter-writing together. Hot apple cider, baked goods, watercolor and writing materials, and stamps are provided. Attendees bring or collect addresses of friends they wish to write to, and the gathering concludes with a walk to a local mailbox.

events.title: Turtling - a Monastic Sporting Event
events.description: An experimental one-hour event described as "the opposite of a running club." Participants experience meditative movement at a slower pace, blending playground and monastery atmospheres, and try a newly invented game built at the venue.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden
events.description: Marked as the final session of the yoga series, this inclusive 'EveryBody Flow' session welcomes those who feel inflexible, have larger bodies, or don't identify with traditional yoga settings, with adaptive practices, good vibes, and a relaxed atmosphere. events.title: Breathe Easy: Group Meditation
events.description: A gathering exploring how voices can open new pathways to connection and expression. Participants may bring songs to share or join in harmonizing; session begins with brief meditation, followed by unstructured singing where attendees teach tunes to the group, with mindful pauses between songs.

events.title: Tarot and Tea in the Garden
events.description: Invites participants to learn the basics of tarot, brush up on intuitive skills, and enjoy some tea, whether new to tarot or experienced. Described as the organization's first-ever Tarot event. Attendees are encouraged to bring a tarot deck if they have one (bonus points for bringing extras to share).

events.title: Creatives Club & Community Jam
events.description: Creatives Club DC provides a space for creatives in the DMV to meet like-minded people and socialize over low-key art-making; participants may bring works in progress or start new projects, including collaborating with Ben and Matthew on Temperance Alley Garden's community mixtape, to be recorded and produced over the course of the year. Part of a recurring weekly series.

events.title: Music in the Garden!
events.description: Final show of the year, featuring TBD, an indie folk collaboration of Tiffany Wimenta and Ben Roberts, and Paul 'Moonshine' Mooney, who blends musical comedy on piano with original folk music on banjo and also works as a violinist and comedian specializing in alternative comedy.

events.title: Garden Volunteer Hours
events.description: Community volunteer session tending shared crops, filling water barrels, and working on side projects together with garden stewards and Farm the District. Everyone welcome, especially first-time visitors; attendees take home a portion of fresh produce.

events.description: The third annual pumpkin carving event, a 3-hour rolling event where attendees are encouraged to wear costumes. Organizers provide snacks, candles, and limited extra supplies, but participants should ideally bring their own pumpkins and carving tools or stencils. Pumpkin painting is offered as an alternative activity.

events.title: Tai Chi
events.description: An invitation to explore the basics of Tai Chi, no prior experience required. Tai Chi is presented as encompassing multiple dimensions: moving meditation, health practice, martial art, energy work, and philosophical practice. Led by Evan Colton, a 20th generation disciple in the Wudang Longmen lineage and a 2x national champion in Tai Chi Push Hands.

events.title: Garden Death Cafe
events.description: Intimate gathering exploring mortality through conversation, discussing death while eating cake and drinking tea in a candlelit setting. Follows a meditation circle; welcomes all attendees to dialogue about this typically avoided subject. The garden's inaugural Death Cafe, part of a broader global initiative encouraging open discourse on death-related topics. Laura Lyster-Mensh, a "Death Doula" from Congressional Cemetery, participates.

events.title: Between the Streets: a Flowerside Chat on the Hidden History of D.C. Alley Life
events.location: 1909 13th Street Northwest, Washington, DC 20009
events.description: Discussion exploring Washington D.C.'s forgotten alleyway communities through urban planning, public health, and architecture. Covers the "parallel worlds" where the city's poorest residents historically built lives and communities, what happened to these spaces, and how this history informs contemporary urban design. Features conversations with Carolyn Swope, a Columbia University doctoral student researching housing and health disparities, and Kim Williams, an architectural historian with D.C.'s Historic Preservation Office and author of "Hidden Alleyways of Washington D.C."

Activities include a meal prepared by a local chef, herbal tea, and Narcan training provided by HIPS. Suggested donations support mutual aid organizations.

events.title: TAG StoryKeeping Circle
events.description: Gathering focused on reflective listening to all that's budding within the Garden ecosystem. Participants may bring a TAG story to share or discover one based on the evening's prompts. Circles occur on the middle Monday of each month; all are welcome to participate fully.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden
events.description: All-levels yoga flow held outdoors in the garden. Community donation-suggested class amid greenery and open sky, geared toward grounding and centering. Bring yourself, water, and a mat; a few extra mats are available on hand.

events.title: Breathe Easy: Group Meditation
events.description: A weekly meditation gathering where participants re-center, connect, and cultivate intentions through guided, silent, and relational practice centered on presence and compassion. Hosted by regulars and guests alike; all levels of experience are welcome, and attendees are invited to bring a practice for the circle to share.

events.title: Donation Harvest for Martha's Table
events.description: Our midweek harvest is donated to Martha's Table through a long-running partnership with Farm the District. Participants are invited to give back to the community; no experience necessary, we'll teach you how to harvest.

events.title: TAG-Along: DC Bike Party!
events.description: Meet up and roll together to DC Bike Party's monthly social ride. Attendees should bring bikes or use bikeshare, gathering at the garden during volunteer harvest hours before departing around 7:50pm.

events.title: Summertime Sadness
events.description: An end-of-summer celebration inviting attendees to revel in all that has been lost to time, featuring poetry sharing, a designated cursing area, a sad catwalk performance, a weeping station, and ceremonial candy cigarette smoking. Attendees are encouraged to dress in melancholic fashion and bring comfort foods.

events.title: Gallery in the Garden
events.description: Exhibition featuring photographer Julia Keane's landscape photography from ecosystems worldwide, designed to inspire environmental stewardship locally. 25% of donations to the artist go to mutual aid efforts in Palestine.

events.title: Morning Hours
events.description: Informal drop-in morning gathering with no structured agenda. Attendees can meditate, walk dogs, read, or enjoy the space; refreshments including coffee and tea may be available.

events.title: Garden Volunteer Hours
events.description: A recurring Saturday volunteer opportunity during the growing season where participants help maintain the community garden with weeding, watering, composting, repairing, crafting, and ongoing side projects. Newcomers receive orientation tours, and volunteers can take home their share of harvested produce.

events.title: Backyard Cookout & Picnic
events.description: Evening gathering in the garden during peak summer with grills going for communal cooking and a chill atmosphere to mingle with neighbors. Attendees encouraged to bring items to share.

events.title: Sonnet Soiree!
events.description: An evening focused on poetry exploration, featuring prompts, mindfulness exercises, and games to craft verses collaboratively and individually. The game-centered event welcomes both experienced poets and newcomers.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden
events.description: All-levels yoga flow held outdoors in the garden. Community donation-suggested class amid greenery and open sky, geared toward grounding and centering. Bring yourself, water, and a mat; a few extra mats are available on hand.

events.title: Donation Harvest for Martha's Table
events.description: Midweek harvest event where produce is donated to Martha's Table through a long-running partnership with Farm the District. Open to all skill levels — organizers teach attendees how to harvest as part of giving back to the community.

events.title: Creatives Club & Community Jam
events.description: An artistic gathering where creatives can work on personal projects together in the garden. Attendees are invited to bring a project they're working on, their instrument of choice, or just come socialize with fellow creatives. Basic art supplies will be available.

events.title: Morning Hours
events.description: Informal drop-in morning gathering with "no group agenda." Attendees can meditate, walk pets, read, or explore the space; "there may even be coffee and tea!" All are welcome.

events.title: Garden Volunteer Hours
events.description: Come get your hands in the soil with partners at Farm the District, every Saturday throughout the growing season. Whether a first-timer or returning veteran, all are needed to help tend the shared backyard garden. Plenty of projects to keep hands busy, including weeding, watering, composting, repairing, crafting, and ongoing side projects — find a partner and choose your own adventure, and take home a share of the communal harvest. An orientation tour is given for newcomers.

events.title: Embodying The Elements: Exploring Nature with Movement
events.description: A playful dance workshop where participants embody various natural elements—roots, water, fire, trees—through guided and improvised movement. Emphasizes self-consent and boundaries; no prior dance experience required. Free event with optional RSVP via Eventbrite; donations to Soul Shine Theater Garden encouraged at the gate.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden
events.description: A grounding evening yoga flow featuring exploration of the expansion and contraction of joy in the body, with vocal toning and self-inquiry practices. Participants should bring themselves, water, and a mat. 100% of proceeds from this donation-suggested class go directly toward Network for Victim Recovery DC (NVRDC).

events.title: Gay's Garden Comedy Showcase
events.description: A standup comedy showcase featuring queer performers from the DMV area, including two comedians (Twink and Doll) competing during the performance. Billed as a night for those who "like dirty, messy, little queers fighting in a kiddy pool."

events.description: A community Shabbat gathering under the stars featuring reflective rituals and communal dining, part of OneTable's national 'Permission to Unplug' series for young adults. Potluck contributions are encouraged and pizza is provided; all are welcome.

events.description: Attendees write (and optionally mail) a letter to a tutor or mentor who impacted their life, while learning how to volunteer to tutor or mentor in their neighborhood. Writing materials, stamps, and access to a local mailbox provided.

events.description: An event centered on practicing written correspondence. Writing materials and stamps are provided on-site, and the activity concludes with mailing letters at a local mailbox. events.title: Neighborhood Screening of "Divisible" by Lizzie Barrett
events.description: Documentary screening examining the history and current impacts of redlining in the United States, highlighting the case of Omaha, Nebraska, and its ongoing effects on communities. Attendees invited to bring blankets and snacks to share; screening begins at dusk.

events.title: Sukkot Shabbat Dinner
events.description: A potluck gathering to celebrate Sukkot, the Jewish harvest festival. Attendees break bread in the Garden's Sukkah, share a meal, and participate in blessings and rituals led by the host, emphasizing community connection and welcoming those without prior knowledge of the holiday. Vegetarian dishes are requested, and guests should label dishes with allergen information.

Includes a birding mindfulness lesson from 5-6pm followed by a musical performance from 6-7:30pm. Co-sponsored by DC Bird Alliance and Feminist Bird Club DMV.

events.description: An evening of live music featuring three performers: Julien Xuereb (French guitarist and singer blending classical, jazz, rock, and chanson styles), SqueazyE (DC-based alternative accordionist), and Adams Morgan Folk Collective (a DC-based group of multi-instrumentalists performing eclectic original songs and traditional folk interpretations). events.title: Pumpkin Carving Party
events.description: The fourth annual pumpkin carving event, a 2-hour rolling event where costumes are encouraged. Attendees should bring their own pumpkin (BYOP) and any stencils/tools needed; organizers supply snacks, candles, and materials for pumpkin painting as an alternative activity. The garden's fourth anniversary will also be celebrated with cake.

events.title: Embodying The Elements: Exploring Nature with Movement
events.description: A playful dance workshop to move your body and connect with the nature that surrounds us. Through movement, attendees explore their relationship with natural elements including roots, water, fire, and trees, emphasizing self-consent, boundaries, and improvisation with play and joy as ways of collective liberation. No prior dance experience required. Free; optional RSVP via Eventbrite; donations to Soul Shine Theater Garden requested at the gate.

events.title: Lunch Hours
events.description: An informal daytime gathering where people working from home or nearby can bring their lunch to the Garden and chat with folks from the neighborhood. Open lunch hours hosted by Nina.

events.title: TAG HouseKeeping Circle
events.description: Monthly operational meeting to plan garden programming for the following month. Attendees bring ideas, discuss event planning and budgeting, and vote on garden policies. HouseKeeping Circles happen at the end of each month as the functional meetings where events are calendared for the next month of programming, spending is approved, and Garden policies can be changed if needed. Official meeting minutes are maintained and shared with the U Street Neighborhood Association.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden

events.title: Queer Black Broadway Walking Tour
events.location: Begins at Howard Theater; ends at Thurst Lounge, 2204 14th Street, Washington, DC
events.description: A 1.5-hour tour celebrating LGBTQ History Month by exploring the U Street Corridor's "Black Broadway" era, covering the neighborhood's role from Prohibition through the Civil Rights Movement as a hub for social justice movements and its vibrant, secretive queer community connected to the Harlem Renaissance and entertainment worlds. Proceeds benefit the Rainbow History Project.

events.title: Paint Your Grief

All materials provided, no prior art experience needed; attendees should bring journal reflections on their grief and reference photos. events.description: A gathering celebrating the intersection of nature and tradition with a Shabbat dinner honoring Sukkot ('z'man simchateinu' - season of our joy). Attendees bless the night, share a potluck meal, and appreciate the fall harvest together amid freshly picked produce and the woven walls of the sukkah. Attendees are invited to bring additional dishes, nonalcoholic beverages, or desserts to share; all are welcome to join the Garden potluck.

events.title: Garden Volunteer Hours
events.description: Recurring Saturday volunteer session at the community garden throughout the growing season, held with partners at Farm the District. Volunteers help with weeding, watering, composting, harvesting, repairing, crafting, and ongoing side projects; volunteers may take home a portion of the harvest, with an orientation tour provided for first-timers.

events.title: Tech Justice Working Group
events.description: Part 2 of a de-googling initiative working group. A collaborative work session for accountability and troubleshooting adoption of alternative digital/analog services as a group. Attendees from the prior month's meeting receive checklists; participants should bring laptops, phones, and journals, and are encouraged to bring friends (no wifi available at venue).
events.possibleExceptionDescription: Part 2 of multi-part working group series

events.title: Staged Reading in the Garden
events.description: An outdoor theater experience presented by Ugly Rhinoceros, featuring actors (Olivia Cholewczynski, Rui Costa, Imaan Khan, and Elijah Rakha-Sheketoff) who remain seated with scripts but perform in character, bringing the play to life with their voices. Attendees may relax in the garden however feels comfortable. Free admission; a suggested $10-$15 donation (optional) is split between the Public Media Bridge Fund, Committee to Protect Journalists, and ACLU.

events.title: National Coming Out Day: Queer Storytelling Circle
events.description: Invites queer individuals across generations to tell their own stories of love, loss, joy, heartbreak, and everything in between, including relationships and coming-out stories. No storytelling experience needed; LGBTQ+ friends and allies welcome.

events.title: Pumpkin Carving & Painting ~ Families Welcome!
events.description: An autumn gathering where families can participate in pumpkin carving and painting activities together. Organizers will provide the paints and some pumpkins and supplies, but additions are welcome. Attendees are told to get ready to be messy.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden
events.description: All-levels donation-suggested yoga flow in the garden amid greenery and open sky, focused on grounding and centering. Bring yourself, water, and a mat (a few extras available on site).

events.title: TAG StoryKeeping Circle
events.description: A monthly gathering focused on reflective listening to all that's budding within the Garden ecosystem. Participants are invited to share narratives from the previous month or participate through evening prompts. Held on the middle Monday of each month; open to all.

events.title: Breathe Easy: Group Meditation
events.description: A weekly meditation community gathering with guided, silent, and relational practice to re-center, connect, and cultivate intentions. Participants warmly welcomed at all experience levels; encouraged to bring a practice for the circle to share.

events.title: Donation Harvest for Martha's Table

events.title: Creatives Club & Community Jam
events.description: Gather for productive artistic time together while the garden is blooming. Bring projects you're working on, musical instruments, or simply come to interact with other creative individuals. Some basic art supplies will be provided.

events.title: Plant Party + TAG's 5th Birthday
events.description: Attendees are invited to come dressed as their favorite plant to celebrate the turn of the seasons and play plant-themed party games; participants can share about their costume or engage in craft activities.

events.title: Garden Volunteer Hours
events.description: Volunteer session with Farm the District partners, held every Saturday throughout the growing season. Welcomes newcomers and returning volunteers for tasks such as weeding, watering, composting, harvesting, repairing, crafting, and side projects. Attendees find a partner, choose their own adventure, and take home harvested produce. Orientation tour provided for first-timers.

events.title: Prayers in the Garden
events.description: An original theater garden creation featuring ritual dance language that weaves together song, poetry, dance, and story sharing in dialogue with the garden and earth. Marks the culmination of Soul Shine Theater Garden's first season, "Wild Faith: Believe. Blossom. Become.," and includes storytelling about local U Street neighborhood history and ancestral honoring. No on-site bathroom (nearest facilities at Brew on U Café or 14th Street Trader Joe's); outdoor, open-air space with limited shade structures. Donations encouraged to support Soul Shine Theater Garden. Part of Sisi's 2025 Artist in Residence program at the venue.

events.title: Garden Death Cafe

events.title: Save a Tree, Plant a Forest
events.description: Ecological restoration event at the Temperance Alley Sanctuary. Participants collect non-native plants for papermaking, transplant native species for attendee giveaways, and plant tree and understory seedlings, while learning how to rescue neighborhood plant seedlings, temporarily pot them, and plant them to thrive. The Sanctuary is described as a new extension of Temperance Alley Garden for quiet observation and cultivating reciprocity with the land — a time capsule of Indigenous stewardship, a native food/micro forest restoring biodiversity, and an ecological classroom.

events.description: An ecological improvisation band, led by Aaron Pond, performs aviary and environmentally inspired improvised music using french horns, bass clarinet, and homemade flutes, wearing homemade outfits featuring giant painted bird heads. Features a bird and nature walk at 5:30pm followed by a live improvised performance at 6pm. Co-sponsored by DC Bird Alliance. No registration required; free with optional donations. No bathroom on site; outdoor, open-air space with limited shade and uneven, unpaved ground; accessible via Metro U Street station or buses 90/96.

"Phone-free event—you'll be asked to put your phone away at the gate." Call 1-844-OFFLINE for more information.

events.title: Turmeric Sun Prints & Bird Meditation
events.description: A morning workshop combining sun printing with turmeric and mindful bird observation. Participants create prints using turmeric, then engage in reflective birding while the prints cure in the sunlight, with an optional baking soda water bath to enhance the prints afterward. Tea is provided and participants may bring their own breakfast.

events.title: Tibetan Sound Healing Journey
events.description: An immersive sound journey using Tibetan singing bowls, flute, voice, and intuitive sound to guide participants into meditation and relaxation, aiming to release tension, calm the nervous system, clear emotional blockages, and restore balance and harmony. No prior meditation experience required.

All identities, expressions, and skill levels are welcome; no dance partner required. Pay-what-you-can with a $10 suggested donation to compensate the salsa instructors; free and open to drop in.

events.title: Calling on Courage Season Opener
events.description: The Soul Shine Theater Garden season 2 opener: play, breathe, hear updates on Sisi's work, and gather in collective joy alongside grief and heartache. Includes an Embodying the Elements movement practice; a presentation on the first Soul Shine Theater Garden after-school residency "Imagining the Elements: Learning History through Nature and Imaginative Play" at Choptank Elementary (covering Maryland landscapes and Harriet Tubman); an introduction to and exploration of Playback Theater (an improvisational form where a person shares a story and an ensemble plays it back through acting/dance/song/music, with a few garden community members invited to share stories); and ends with a North Star-themed arts and crafts activity, plus an invitation to Sisi's next performance at Wild Seed Production's Juneteenth event.

events.title: Offline Connections CHALLENGE WEEK: Talking to Strangers
events.description: An Attention Sphere gathering exploring reconnecting through face-to-face interactions. Organizers note "conversations with strangers are a portal to fascinating, funny, beautiful worlds" and that phone culture has shifted how people meet. The session kicks off a week-long challenge series encouraging authentic offline connection, presence, and vulnerability; the shy, socially anxious, and awkward are especially encouraged to attend. A follow-up celebration event occurs on a later date for participants to reconvene and share experiences from the week.

events.title: Drop in to the Plant Doctor with LJPC
events.description: A drop-in session for neighbors seeking houseplant guidance. Attendees can ask questions about watering, sunlight placement, and leaf issues, with plant care professionals providing a "prescription for keeping your plant happy and healthy."
hosts: Jules, Mary, and the Little Jungle Plant Company

events.title: Do you wanna play?
events.description: A four-part play series exploring joy through the elements—water, air, earth, and fire. Participants play like the elements, give play offerings to the elements, and play with the elements; every being benefits from play, and attendees are encouraged to embrace imaginative, vibrant engagement through being both serious and silly. Drop-in format.

events.description: A monthly gathering for reflective listening to all that's budding within the Garden ecosystem, described as a rearview mirror where stories can be shared in community. Participants share stories from the previous month or respond to evening prompts. Held on the middle Monday of each month; open to all.

events.description: A garden gathering to discuss the latest season of Love Island USA and explore broader cultural themes including the panopticon, Gen Z dating, and misogyny (and misogynoir) in dating, plus reactions to the season's finalists.

events.location: Harrison Park, 1330 V Street Northwest, Washington, DC 20009
events.description: An outdoor, free, family-friendly screening of Zootopia 2 as part of the U Street Movie Series. events.title: Creatives Club & Community Jam
events.description: Gather for productive artistic time together while the garden is blooming. Bring your own projects, musical instruments, or simply join to connect with other artists. Basic art materials made available at the venue.

events.title: Henna in the Garden: From Leaves to Skin
events.description: Interactive workshop where participants learn to apply and receive henna with the garden community, for both a sensory and creative experience. Designs are inspired by the plant life and objects around the garden. No prior experience required; those concerned about staining are advised to wear dark clothing.

events.title: Garden Volunteer Hours
events.description: Recurring Saturday volunteer opportunity throughout the growing season in partnership with Farm the District. Newcomers and experienced volunteers help with weeding, watering, composting, harvesting, repairing, crafting, and ongoing side projects; Farm Leads guide daily tasks and participants may come and go as they please. Orientation tours available for first-time attendees.

events.title: Collecting, Preserving and Nurturing Ourselves and our Histories
events.description: A session exploring memory, storytelling, and archiving through a grounding, connection-centered experience. Activities include a garden walkthrough highlighting the space's evolution and the Temperance Alley Sanctuary rewilding efforts, guided conversation on preserving history, and an arts-based reflection component. Attendees are encouraged to bring photos for collaging and personal archiving materials if they wish to participate in the creative activities.

events.title: Offline Connections CHALLENGE WEEK: Talking to Strangers
events.description: A reflection/celebration gathering for participants of the Offline Connections Challenge Week. Attendees debrief and swap stories about the people they met while engaging offline instead of using their phones during the preceding week.
events.possibleExceptionDescription: Reuses kickoff event's title, is closing session

events.title: 12hr Film Festival: NOW SCREENING!
events.description: A public screening of the short films shot within the previous 12 hours. Shorts will be shown, praises lauded, and awards given; snacks, drinks and great company provided. Films must be submitted by 8pm to abide by the 12-hour rule. Prizes awarded include Most Niche DC Reference, Best Temperance Alley Garden Reference, Most Daring, Most Experimental, Silliest Dance Sequence, and Most Whimsical.

events.title: 12 hr Film Festival: TAG in to make Film in a Day
events.description: Neighbors meet at Temperance Alley Garden (TAG), form groups, and brainstorm ideas for short films, then disperse to spend the day writing, filming, and editing a roughly 3-minute short around the city on a "zero budget" using phones and apps like iMovie/Canva. No filmmaking experience necessary; participants can fully engage or contribute minimally. The day concludes at 7:30 PM back at the garden for a screening (films must be submitted by 8:00 PM to abide by the 12-hour rule), with prizes awarded including Most Niche DC Reference, Best Temperance Alley Garden Reference, Most Daring, Most Experimental, Silliest Dance Sequence, and Most Whimsical.

events.title: Dream in the Stream: Tin Fish Talk and Taste
events.description: A gathering for participants of all experience levels to sample different tins and pairings while discussing our connection to the food we eat on a local and international level. Attendees may bring snacks or non-alcoholic beverages to complement the tinned fish offerings.

events.title: Monday Morning Musings
events.description: A weekly Monday gathering offering a mindful moment of reset and reconnection at the Garden. Attendees sip something warm, connect with neighbors, and ease into the day, with designated spaces for socializing and quiet reflection or journaling. Drop-in basis, no materials required.

events.title: Yoga in the Garden
events.description: All-levels yoga flow in the garden. Community donation-suggested class amid greenery and open sky, meant for grounding and centering. Bring yourself, water, and a mat (a few extras on hand).

events.title: Scrap to Sculpture: Make Your Own Wire Flower!
events.description: A hands-on workshop where participants learn to combine wires, basic masking tape, and paint to make a funky floral sculpture that sits on your head, repurposing scrap materials.

events.title: Breathe Easy: Group Meditation
events.description: A weekly meditation gathering where participants re-center, connect, and cultivate intentions through guided, silent, and relational practice. All experience levels are welcome, and attendees are encouraged to bring a practice for the circle to share.

events.title: Volunteer Training and Appreciation with EC4DC
events.description: EC4DC-led volunteer training for tabling activities. The 30-minute session covers de-escalation, tabling best practices, mutual aid, and how EC/Plan B works, and certifies participants to table around DC. Refreshments provided.

events.title: Climate Sketchbook Club
events.description: An evening combining creative expression with environmental dialogue. Participants pair up to sketch garden objects while discussing planetary connection and ecological stewardship; no art skills or prior knowledge needed. Attendees may optionally bring their own art supplies.

events.title: Outdoor Screening: "Showing Up"
events.description: Free outdoor screening of Kelly Reichardt's film "Showing Up," described as a vibrant and captivatingly funny portrait of art and craft following a sculptor balancing her creative pursuits with family and community relationships. Attendees arrive at 7:30 PM to socialize; the screening begins at dusk. Bring chairs and blankets; no admission fee. Screening made possible by A24.
events.possibleExceptionDescription: One-time special screening, not regular series

events.title: Introduction to Acting: From Text to Performance
events.description: An introductory acting workshop requiring no prior experience, covering physical and vocal warm-ups from international theatrical traditions, text analysis fundamentals, and improvisation exercises exploring dramatic conflict. Participants progress through contentless scenes and conclude with scene presentations. Co-host Nick is a director of contemporary performance specializing in devised theatre and reimaginings of classic texts, with an MFA from UC San Diego.

events.title: Garden Volunteer Hours
events.description: Recurring Saturday volunteer opportunity during the growing season in partnership with Farm the District. Open to all experience levels, with activities including weeding, watering, composting, harvesting, repairing, crafting, and ongoing side projects. Farm Leads provide instruction; participants may arrive and depart flexibly, and newcomers receive an orientation tour.

events.title: Scripting Worlds: Feel & Flow Writing Workshop
events.description: A creative gathering designed to help participants transition from busy modes into reflective states through body-based practices, followed by unstructured writing and illustration time. The workshop emphasizes "permission without a pressing need to finish" rather than productivity, offering space for personal reflection, processing difficult experiences, or imagining future possibilities. No prior writing or artistic experience required. Attendees should bring a beverage of choice and a comfortable seating item (mat, blanket, chair, or pillow).

events.title: Answers thru Alleyways: What makes a space feel safe?
events.description: A monthly discussion series held the last Thursday of the month, exploring how public spaces connect to our daily lives and inviting participants to think critically about how we build the world around us. Sessions feature introductory readings from psychology, urbanism, sociology, public health, and art. No prior knowledge needed; light readings provided day-of.

events.title: Music in the Garden!
events.description: An informal musical gathering combining elements of a house show and barn jam, centered on live performances in a garden setting. Attendees are welcome to bring blankets and shareable food items. Free and open to the public; monetary contributions to performers are welcomed but not required.

events.title: Garden Volunteer Hours
events.description: Recurring Saturday community gardening event in partnership with Farm the District. Volunteers help with weeding, watering, composting, harvesting, repairing, crafting, and ongoing side projects; the page notes "all are needed to help tend to our shared backyard," with Farm Leads guiding daily tasks. Participants may come and go as they please; orientation tours provided for newcomers.

events.title: No-Buy August: An Introduction
events.description: Kickoff for a No-Buy or Low-Buy August challenge, inviting participants to discuss overconsumption amid algorithmic advertising and industrial pollution, set personal goals, and reconvene at month's end for a closing reflection. Those unable to attend the opening session may still join the challenge and the closing meeting.

artefacts.artefact: The No Cheese Temperance Alley Singalong Zine (Pt 1)
artefacts.description: Zine from Music in the Garden ft. No Cheese
The first TAG events menu, originally fastened directly to the fence
Sign from the Music in the Garden where we introduced the paytato, an NFC potato to encourage donations to the musicians, which raised over $200
Sally invited us to choose an object from the garden, imagine that it has magical powers that will help you with your intentions for the second half of the year, and write an advertisement for it
Music in the Garden attendees wrote their ideas for what we should do at TAG while we have the leaser

artefacts.event: Haiku Garden Walk & Book Launch with Downtown Dharma
artefacts.description: Participants wrote haikus about and to the garden

artefacts.event: Haiku Garden Walk & Book Launch with Downtown Dharma
artefacts.description: Haiku book donated by Downtown Dharma, a collection of poems written on haiku hikes around DC
Zine + worksheet to help participants find a healthier relationship to digital media
Introducing the Attention Sphere series at TAG, a wonderful outgrowth of all the attention activism energy that was generated during the first year of the Month Offline challenge in DC
Sticker pack by Wild Seed Productions, from the programming they are hosting for students near the birthplace of Harriet Tubman, a beautiful exploration of freedom and our spiritual "north stars"

Text from a land acknowledgement ceremony, which was read aloud in the Story Circle, a special christening for the Garden because Virginia Ali of Ben's Chili Bowl was in attendance and shared her memories of Dr. King's work and presence in the U Street Neighborhood

Brainstorming cards from a community conversation after TAG was first offered a lease extension, we asked participants to reflect on what the Garden has been so far and what it might be in the future, in an effort to be intentional about continuing the project instead of walking mindlessly into the future, keeping in mind that the whole thing began as a Covid response and that its reason for being might need to change in the post-pandemic environment

artefacts.event: Mending Workshop: Break Up with Fast Fashion
artefacts.description: TAG encouraged people to break up with fast fashion and practice maintenance & care, sometimes hosting mending events directly after clothing swaps so that people could make the clothes their own

artefacts.artefact: About the Order of Good Death
artefacts.description: Resources from The Order of Good Death from the very first Death Cafe hosted at TAG in collaboration with the amazing Laura (death doula at Congressional Cemetery), a huge turnout despite extremely cold weather, satisfying a real hunger for conversation about death
Worksheets designed by Wild Seed Productions, helping kids define their spiritual north stars, inspired by Harriet Tubman's underground railroad

From the School of Radical Attention, bringing the attention activism movement to Temperance Alley Garden, we introduced ourselves by sharing the line that stood out to us most and began to think of TAG as an "attention sanctuary"

artefacts.event: PLAY Jam: Improv in the Garden
artefacts.description: Run of show for improv event, a series exploring the importance of play
